cannotMixTabsGroupsFoldersDuringDrag.message: It's not possible to mix tabs, groups and folders when dragging. This is because, for example, you can't drag a folder into the same places you can drag a tab.

encryptedSyncAndBackupComingSoonMessage.message:
Coming soon: You will be able to sync your OneTab data across multiple browser profiles and computers.
OneTab has been completely rewritten so that changes made on one device will instantly appear on all other devices.
If you use this feature, your data will be end-to-end encrypted with high-strength AES-256 encryption. This makes it impossible for the OneTab developers to see the tabs you have backed up or synced between devices, because only you have the encryption key.

lockMenuItemHelp.message: When a group is locked, you cannot delete or re-order items within the group. When opening a locked group, automatic actions do not apply. See the Options page for information about automatic actions.

lockedGroupUnlockHelp.message: To unlock, use the dropdown menu. When a group is locked, you cannot delete the group or its tabs, and tabs cannot be reordered.

optionDiscardNonActiveTabOnOpenHeading.message: Save memory (RAM) immediately when tabs are first opened:

optionDiscardNonActiveTabOnOpenNote.message: If this setting is enabled, it will immediately unload tabs from memory when you first open them from your OneTab list. This is useful if you frequently open dozens of tabs at once, as it will prevent them all from being loaded into memory until you access them. If this setting is disabled, many web browsers will still automatically choose to unload tabs from memory if you are not currently using them.

optionDisplayContextMenuHeading.message: Right-click inside a web page to access the OneTab context menu:

optionDisplayContextMenuNote.message: If you disable the menu, you will not be able to access the "Exclude website from OneTab" feature

optionMoveOneTabToCurrentWindowOnOpenFal.message: Switch to the window where the OneTab tab is already open

optionMoveOneTabToCurrentWindowOnOpenTru.message: Move any existing OneTab tab to the current window

optionStartupLaunchDisplay.message: Open OneTab whenever you start your web browser

optionStartupLaunchNoneDesc3.message: To open OneTab manually, click on the OneTab icon on your browser toolbar, and choose 'Open OneTab'. Alternatively, you can right-click inside any web page, and click 'Open OneTab' inside the OneTab menu.

permissionRequiredPleaseTryAgain.message: To create a shared page that can be accessed from any computer, permission is required to coordinate with the OneTab web server. Please try again.

placeholderExplanation.message: Due to web browser security rules, OneTab is not allowed to directly restore this page. Please copy your URL and paste it into your browser's address bar.

plainTextLineFormat.message: If you paste plain text, ensure that each URL has a protocol specified (https://wikipedia.org, instead of just wikipedia.org). Each line should be in the following format:

popupSlashShortcutInfo.message: When inside the OneTab action popup (which appears when you click the OneTab icon), press the slash key to open the destination chooser and focus the search box.

privacyAssuranceDetail.message: Your list of tabs is never transmitted or disclosed to either the OneTab developers or any other party. The only exception to this is if you intentionally use the 'share as a web page' feature to upload your list of tabs into a web page in order to share them with others.

privacyFirstMessage.message:
OneTab has been designed to protect your private data. Your tabs are only stored on your computer, and are never shared with the OneTab developers or any other party.
If you use our backup/sync feature, your data will be end-to-end encrypted with high-strength AES-256 encryption. This makes it impossible for the OneTab developers to see the tabs you have backed up or synced between devices, because only you have the encryption key. 
The only exception is if you intentionally decide to share some of your tabs with other people using our "Share as a web page" feature.
We believe in Privacy First.
